commands.name: apt
commands.description: High-level package manager for Debian-based systems

patterns.text: update the list of available packages
patterns.explanation: Refreshes the package index from configured repositories

patterns.text: upgrade all installed packages
patterns.explanation: Installs the newest versions of all currently installed packages

patterns.text: install a package
patterns.explanation: Downloads and installs nginx and its dependencies

patterns.text: remove a package but keep its config files
patterns.explanation: Uninstalls the package but leaves configuration files

patterns.text: search for a package by name or description
patterns.explanation: Searches the package database for matching packages

patterns.text: install a package without prompts in a script
patterns.explanation: Non-interactive install suitable for automation and Dockerfiles

patterns.text: completely remove a package and its config
patterns.explanation: Removes the package along with all configuration files

patterns.text: clean up downloaded package files
patterns.explanation: Removes cached .deb files to free disk space

patterns.text: remove unused dependencies
patterns.explanation: Uninstalls packages that were installed as dependencies but are no longer needed

patterns.text: do a distribution upgrade
patterns.explanation: Upgrades packages and handles dependency changes intelligently

patterns.text: install a local .deb package file
patterns.explanation: Installs a package directly from a .deb file

patterns.text: list all installed packages
patterns.explanation: Shows every installed package with version and description

patterns.text: find which package owns a file
patterns.explanation: Identifies which package installed the given file

patterns.text: list files installed by a package
patterns.explanation: Shows every file installed by the nginx package

commands.name: yum
commands.description: Package manager for RHEL/CentOS 7 and older

patterns.text: install a package on CentOS or RHEL
patterns.explanation: Installs Apache HTTP server and its dependencies

patterns.text: update all packages
patterns.explanation: Upgrades all packages to the latest available versions

patterns.text: search for a package
patterns.explanation: Searches package names and descriptions for matches

patterns.text: remove a package
patterns.explanation: Uninstalls the specified package

patterns.text: list installed packages
patterns.explanation: Shows all currently installed packages

commands.name: dnf
commands.description: Next-generation package manager for Fedora and RHEL 8+

patterns.text: install a package on Fedora
patterns.explanation: Installs the GCC compiler and its dependencies

patterns.text: search for available packages
patterns.explanation: Searches repositories for packages matching the query

patterns.text: show info about a package
patterns.explanation: Displays details like version, size, and description

patterns.text: remove a package and its unused dependencies
patterns.explanation: Removes the package and any orphaned dependencies

patterns.text: list enabled repositories
patterns.explanation: Shows all currently enabled package repositories

patterns.text: install a package on Arch Linux
patterns.explanation: Syncs and installs the package from the repositories

patterns.text: update all installed packages
patterns.explanation: Synchronizes databases and upgrades all packages

patterns.text: search for a package
patterns.explanation: Searches the sync databases for matching packages

patterns.text: remove a package and its unused dependencies
patterns.explanation: Removes the package, its config, and orphaned dependencies

patterns.text: list all explicitly installed packages
patterns.explanation: Shows packages that were explicitly installed by the user

commands.name: brew
commands.description: Package manager for macOS and Linux (Homebrew)

patterns.text: install a command-line tool on macOS
patterns.explanation: Downloads and installs the formula

patterns.text: install a macOS GUI application
patterns.explanation: Installs a desktop application as a cask

patterns.text: update Homebrew and all installed packages
patterns.explanation: Fetches the latest formulae and upgrades all packages

patterns.text: search for a package in Homebrew
patterns.explanation: Searches for formulae and casks matching the query

patterns.text: uninstall a package and clean up
patterns.explanation: Removes the formula and deletes old cached downloads

patterns.text: see what is wrong with your Homebrew setup
patterns.explanation: Runs diagnostics for permissions, PATH, and outdated Xcode CLT

patterns.text: list only top-level installed formulas you asked for
patterns.explanation: Omits dependencies pulled in automatically

patterns.text: show the dependency tree of a formula
patterns.explanation: Visualizes recursive dependencies before a large install

patterns.text: reproduce a machine from a Brewfile
patterns.explanation: Installs everything listed in brew bundle format for dotfiles repos

patterns.text: manage background services installed by brew
patterns.explanation: Starts redis as a user LaunchAgent on macOS

commands.name: snap
commands.description: Package manager for snap packages on Ubuntu/Linux

patterns.text: install a snap package
patterns.explanation: Installs the snap from the Snap Store

patterns.text: install a classic snap without confinement
patterns.explanation: Installs VS Code in classic mode with full system access

patterns.text: list installed snaps
patterns.explanation: Shows all installed snap packages and their versions

patterns.text: update all snap packages
patterns.explanation: Updates all installed snaps to their latest versions

patterns.text: install a Flatpak application
patterns.explanation: Installs GIMP from the Flathub repository

patterns.text: run a Flatpak application
patterns.explanation: Launches the installed Flatpak application

patterns.text: list installed Flatpak apps
patterns.explanation: Shows all installed Flatpak applications and runtimes

patterns.text: update all Flatpak applications
patterns.explanation: Checks for and installs updates for all Flatpak apps

patterns.text: install a Python package
patterns.explanation: Downloads and installs the package from PyPI

patterns.text: install packages from a requirements file
patterns.explanation: Installs all packages listed in the requirements file

patterns.text: upgrade a package to the latest version
patterns.explanation: Updates the specified package to the newest version

patterns.text: list all installed Python packages
patterns.explanation: Shows all installed packages and their versions

patterns.text: freeze installed packages into a requirements file
patterns.explanation: Exports exact package versions for reproducibility

patterns.text: install a package for Python 3 specifically
patterns.explanation: Installs the package into the Python 3 environment

patterns.text: install a package for the current user only
patterns.explanation: Installs to the user directory without needing sudo

patterns.text: show details about an installed package
patterns.explanation: Displays version, location, dependencies, and metadata

patterns.text: uninstall a Python package
patterns.explanation: Removes the specified package

patterns.text: install all project dependencies
patterns.explanation: Reads package.json and installs all listed dependencies

patterns.text: add a new package to the project
patterns.explanation: Installs express and adds it to package.json

patterns.text: install a package as a dev dependency
patterns.explanation: Adds jest to devDependencies in package.json

patterns.text: install a package globally
patterns.explanation: Installs the package system-wide for CLI usage

patterns.text: run a script defined in package.json
patterns.explanation: Executes the build script from the scripts section

commands.name: npx
commands.description: Execute npm packages without installing them globally, run one-off tools and scaffolding commands

patterns.text: start a quick local web server to serve files
patterns.explanation: Serves current directory on port 8080 with directory listing

patterns.text: start a static file server with CORS enabled
patterns.explanation: Serves current directory on port 3000 with CORS headers

patterns.text: start a mock REST API server from a JSON file
patterns.explanation: Creates a full REST API from a JSON file with CRUD endpoints

patterns.text: expose local server to the internet with a public URL
patterns.explanation: Creates a public tunnel URL pointing to your local port 3000

patterns.text: create a new Next.js application
patterns.explanation: Scaffolds a new Next.js project with the latest template

patterns.text: create a new Vite project with React and TypeScript
patterns.explanation: Scaffolds a Vite project with React and TypeScript configured

patterns.text: create a new React Native Expo app
patterns.explanation: Scaffolds a new Expo React Native project

patterns.text: clone a git repo without git history as a template
patterns.explanation: Downloads repo contents without .git history, great for templates

patterns.text: create a new React app with Create React App
patterns.explanation: Scaffolds a React project with TypeScript template

patterns.text: format all code files with Prettier
patterns.explanation: Auto-formats all supported files in the current directory

patterns.text: check code formatting without modifying files
patterns.explanation: Reports which files are not formatted without changing them

patterns.text: lint all JavaScript and TypeScript files
patterns.explanation: Runs ESLint and auto-fixes what it can across JS/TS files

patterns.text: initialize a TypeScript configuration file
patterns.explanation: Creates a tsconfig.json with default compiler options

patterns.text: type check TypeScript project without emitting files
patterns.explanation: Runs TypeScript compiler in check-only mode

patterns.text: detect circular dependencies in a project
patterns.explanation: Finds circular import chains in TypeScript/JavaScript files

patterns.text: find unused dependencies in package.json
patterns.explanation: Analyzes imports and reports packages in package.json that are unused

patterns.text: kill whatever process is running on a specific port
patterns.explanation: Finds and kills the process occupying port 3000

patterns.text: move files to trash instead of permanently deleting
patterns.explanation: Moves files to system trash so they can be recovered

patterns.text: cross-platform recursive directory removal like rm -rf
patterns.explanation: Deletes directories recursively, works on Windows too

patterns.text: generate a QR code in the terminal
patterns.explanation: Renders a QR code as ASCII art in the terminal

patterns.text: show simplified man page for a command
patterns.explanation: Shows practical examples for a command from the tldr-pages project

patterns.text: open a URL or file from the command line
patterns.explanation: Opens URL in default browser or file in default application

patterns.text: initialize Prisma ORM in a project
patterns.explanation: Creates prisma/schema.prisma and .env with database URL placeholder

patterns.text: run Prisma database migrations
patterns.explanation: Creates and applies a migration from schema changes

patterns.text: open Prisma Studio database browser
patterns.explanation: Opens a web UI to browse and edit database records

patterns.text: generate Drizzle ORM database migration
patterns.explanation: Generates SQL migration files from Drizzle schema changes

patterns.text: push Drizzle schema directly to database without migration
patterns.explanation: Syncs schema to database directly, useful for prototyping

patterns.text: check for outdated npm packages and update package.json
patterns.explanation: Updates all version ranges in package.json to latest

patterns.text: check bundle size impact of a package before installing
patterns.explanation: Shows minified, gzipped, and download size of a package

patterns.text: initialize git hooks with Husky
patterns.explanation: Sets up Husky and creates a pre-commit hook template

patterns.text: automate npm package release with version bump
patterns.explanation: Bumps version, creates changelog, git tag, and publishes to npm

patterns.text: analyze webpack bundle and visualize module sizes
patterns.explanation: Opens interactive treemap of bundle contents from webpack stats

patterns.text: show system environment info for bug reports
patterns.explanation: Prints OS, Node, npm, browsers, and installed packages info

patterns.text: list all package licenses in a project
patterns.explanation: Scans node_modules and lists license types used

patterns.text: run a Lighthouse performance audit on a URL
patterns.explanation: Generates a performance, accessibility, and SEO audit report

patterns.text: test internet connection speed from terminal
patterns.explanation: Runs a speed test and shows download/upload speeds

patterns.text: run a command with environment variables cross-platform
patterns.explanation: Sets environment variables in a way that works on Windows and Unix

patterns.text: run TypeScript file directly without compiling
patterns.explanation: Executes TypeScript with ESM support, no tsconfig needed

patterns.text: auto-restart node server on file changes
patterns.explanation: Watches for file changes and automatically restarts the process

patterns.text: initialize Storybook in a project
patterns.explanation: Detects framework and sets up Storybook with example stories

patterns.text: initialize Tailwind CSS configuration
patterns.explanation: Creates tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js files

patterns.text: run multiple npm scripts in parallel
patterns.explanation: Runs multiple commands simultaneously with combined output

patterns.text: optimize SVG files reducing their size
patterns.explanation: Removes metadata and optimizes SVG paths to reduce file size

patterns.text: run a specific version of a node package
patterns.explanation: Temporarily uses a specific Node.js version for one command

patterns.text: execute a package from a GitHub repository directly
patterns.explanation: Downloads and runs a package directly from a GitHub repo

commands.name: yarn
commands.description: Fast, reliable, and secure dependency management for JavaScript

patterns.text: install all project dependencies with Yarn
patterns.explanation: Installs dependencies from yarn.lock for deterministic builds

patterns.text: add a package to the project
patterns.explanation: Installs lodash and adds it to package.json

patterns.text: add a dev dependency
patterns.explanation: Adds eslint as a development dependency

patterns.text: remove a package
patterns.explanation: Uninstalls the package and updates package.json

patterns.text: upgrade all packages to their latest versions
patterns.explanation: Updates all dependencies to the newest available versions

patterns.text: install a Ruby gem
patterns.explanation: Downloads and installs the Rails gem

patterns.text: list installed gems
patterns.explanation: Shows all locally installed gems and their versions

patterns.text: update a gem to the latest version
patterns.explanation: Upgrades bundler to the newest available version

patterns.text: uninstall a gem
patterns.explanation: Removes the specified gem from the system

commands.name: cargo
commands.description: Package manager and build system for Rust

patterns.text: create a new Rust project
patterns.explanation: Scaffolds a new Rust binary project with Cargo.toml

patterns.text: build the Rust project
patterns.explanation: Compiles the project with optimizations enabled

patterns.text: run the Rust project
patterns.explanation: Compiles and immediately executes the binary

patterns.text: install a Rust binary crate globally
patterns.explanation: Compiles and installs the binary to ~/.cargo/bin

patterns.text: run tests for the project
patterns.explanation: Compiles and runs all tests in the project

commands.name: go install
commands.description: Compile and install Go packages and binaries

patterns.text: install a Go tool globally
patterns.explanation: Downloads, compiles, and installs the binary to GOPATH/bin

patterns.text: install a specific version of a Go tool
patterns.explanation: Installs a pinned version of the linter

patterns.text: install all dependencies for a Go module
patterns.explanation: Downloads all module dependencies to the local cache

patterns.text: add a dependency to a Go project
patterns.explanation: Adds the package as a dependency and downloads it

patterns.explanation: Removes unused dependencies and adds missing ones
